commitMessage:
Bug 570567 - [15] "New Interface" and "New Class" wizards to be enhanced
to support "non-sealed" modifier

This change handles the case where if a sealed supertype is selected and
'sealed' radio button is selected and when the sealed supertype is
removed, the  'sealed' radio button is deselected automatically. This
should not happen as sealed is a valid modifier here.

Change-Id: I0fbb7fa5f4a6063b7041b42bbaec0ba7b601a7ae
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/186445
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: The changes modify the internal logic and UI behavior of Eclipse JDT wizards to support new language features.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently does not support structural changes like adding new 'if' blocks, conditionally wrapping existing statements in 'if' blocks, or creating new private helper methods. The replacement of a custom method call with an `isEnabled` check is also tied deeply to internal wizard state management, making it hard to generalize.
summary: This commit modifies the internal logic of the 'New Type' wizard page to correctly handle 'non-sealed' modifiers, especially when interacting with sealed supertypes. The changes involve modifying complex conditional expressions, adding new 'if' blocks, and introducing a new private helper method. These are structural and behavioral changes to internal UI logic and cannot be expressed as simple pattern-based DSL rules. The commit aligns with Java 15/17 features (Sealed Classes).

commitMessage:
Bug 576517 - NewClassWizard should throw an error if record is selected
as a super class

Change-Id: Ibdf004f5c9ef971d6c390848a41407ae4fa7cd70
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/186448
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: The changes introduce new validation logic and error messages within an Eclipse JDT wizard.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support adding new NLS message keys or inserting new 'if' blocks with complex control flow (e.g., 'try-catch' blocks and 'return' statements).
summary: This commit adds validation to the 'New Class' wizard to prevent the selection of a `record` as a superclass, as records are implicitly final. The changes involve adding a new NLS message key and introducing a new 'if' block with `fSuperClass.isRecord()` check, error reporting, and a 'try-catch' block. These are structural additions of validation logic and cannot be expressed as simple pattern-based DSL rules. The `isRecord()` method was introduced in Java 16.

commitMessage:
Bug 576535 - SurroundWithTryWithResources fails certain scenarios

- add JVM 1.8 check in QuickAssistProcessor add surround with
  try-with-resources method
- add protection to SurroundWithTryWithResourcesAnalyzer calls to
  get exceptions and caught exceptions to guard against
  fEnclosingNode being set to null
- in SurroundWithTryWithResourcesRefactoring look for the enclosing
  Block instead of a BodyDeclaration
- also fix findNodesInRange() method to just look for statements
  that have the original ast node as their parent and have
  components that fall within the given range

Change-Id: I4b05d9bdd26626824e5e511fbd631e190a85989a
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/186317
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The changes are internal bug fixes and refinements for an existing JDT quick assist, involving complex logical and structural modifications.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support adding new 'if' blocks at the beginning of methods, replacing variable declarations with complex ternary expressions involving multiple 'instanceof' checks and new internal utility methods (like `ASTNodes.getFirstAncestorOrNull`, which is not an exposed guard). These are complex logical and structural changes.
summary: This commit fixes several issues in the 'Surround With Try-With-Resources' quick assist. This includes adding a Java 8 version check for the quick assist's availability, and refactoring how parent AST nodes are found using a complex ternary expression, including `instanceof` checks and a call to `ASTNodes.getFirstAncestorOrNull()`. These are internal bug fixes and refinements involving structural code additions and complex logical restructuring, which cannot be expressed as simple pattern-based DSL rules.

These structural changes are not directly expressible by the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 533524 - [call hierarchy] call hierarchy should show lambdas

Added separated 'declaration' nodes for lambda declarations in the Call
Hierarchy view.

Change-Id: I89ad378ec6d3fa6ce8938de72d9c6942024437a4
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/185428
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Noopur Gupta <noopur_gupta@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-by: Zsombor <gzsombor@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Andrey Loskutov <loskutov@gmx.de>
categoryReason: This pattern adapts an existing Eclipse JDT UI component (Call Hierarchy) to better integrate with and display Java 8 language features (lambdas), representing an internal Eclipse API modernization.
summary: This commit enhances the Eclipse JDT Call Hierarchy to display lambda declarations. While most changes are structural (adding new methods, enums, and complex `if/else` blocks), one specific modification is a direct 1:1 boolean expression replacement: `type.isAnonymous()` is extended to `type.isAnonymous() || type.isLambda()`. This pattern is reusable and directly implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule, improving internal JDT UI consistency with Java 8 features. Lambdas were introduced in Java 8, so no explicit `sourceVersionGE` guard is needed beyond the baseline.

commitMessage:
Bug 321464 - Quick fix for import of member class does not work

- modify UnresolvedElementsSubProcessor.createTypeRefChangeProposal()
  method to check if a type it determines requires an import is actually
  a nested class in the same compilation unit in which case simply
  change the reference to fully qualified
- add new QualifyTypeProposal class
- add new test to UnresolvedTypesQuickFixTest

Change-Id: I60d73b436dfcf4b862cb92126d85e525617532da
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/186734
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit modifies JDT's internal quick-fix logic to correctly handle import proposals for nested classes.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need capabilities to perform complex type binding resolution (e.g., `IType.getFullyQualifiedName()`, `ICompilationUnit.getAllTypes()`) and dynamic name construction based on JDT model elements, which are currently not available. It would also need support for more advanced contextual guards to determine if a simple name refers to an inner class within the same compilation unit.
summary: This commit enhances JDT's quick-fix mechanism by introducing a new `QualifyTypeProposal` for unresolved simple names that refer to nested classes within the same compilation unit. The logic involves iterating through all types in the compilation unit and resolving fully qualified names to determine the correct proposal. This decision-making process requires advanced JDT model element access and type resolution capabilities that are beyond the current TriggerPattern DSL, classifying it as RED. The actual rewrite (simple name to qualified name) is simple, but the condition for application is complex.

commitMessage:
Bug 576980 - Use LF as line endings, not CR-LF

The large majority of other files uses plain LF.

Some test resources and some forceQualifierUpdate.txt files also use
CR-LF as line endings. These files are left unchanged.

NewWizardMessage.properties and NewTypeWizardPage.java even used mixed
line endings; most lines using plain LF, but a few using CR-LF. These
are normalized to LF only.

Change-Id: I301ec44b8f24e2960b05163e1966a06a7d3c83c8
Signed-off-by: Thomas Wolf <thomas.wolf@paranor.ch>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/187191
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Andrey Loskutov <loskutov@gmx.de>
irrelevantReason: The commit changes line endings from CR-LF to LF across files. This is a file-level formatting concern, not an AST-based code transformation pattern that the TriggerPattern DSL is designed to address. The DSL operates on Java code syntax and semantics, not file encoding or line ending characteristics.
summary: This commit changes line endings in `.properties` and `.java` files from CR-LF to LF. This is a file-level formatting change, not a code transformation pattern that can be generalized or implemented using the TriggerPattern DSL, which operates on abstract syntax trees.

commitMessage:
Bug 577040 - Fix cursor positioning in lambda completion

Fix the formatting issue by avoiding going into link mode when lambda
doesn't have parameters.

Change-Id: Idc4ccf49752c63d21776cd033b1398a0127ccfa8
Signed-off-by: Gayan Perera <gayanper@gmail.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/187285
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Lars Vogel <Lars.Vogel@vogella.com>
categoryReason: The change is an internal bug fix within an Eclipse JDT UI component, modifying conditional logic to improve correctness.
summary: The commit fixes a cursor positioning bug in lambda completion by modifying an `if` condition. It extends an existing boolean expression by adding `fArgumentOffsets.length > 0`, ensuring the link mode is only entered when the lambda actually has parameters. This is a direct boolean expression replacement, suitable for a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule, specifically targeting an internal JDT UI bug fix. The rule is guarded by `inClass` to ensure it applies to the correct internal component, and `sourceVersionGE(8)` because lambdas were introduced in Java 8.

commitMessage:
Bug 447798: Open Type dialog accepts filenames and stacktraces.

If a string is pasted into the Open Type dialog it is automatically
cleaned up to the included type name by best effort.

Change-Id: Ibc79ea1ed3a17d95cd66d2c4388d5d4803250bb7
Signed-off-by: Joerg Kubitz <jkubitz-eclipse@gmx.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/186496
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Noopur Gupta <noopur_gupta@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: This change implements a specific user interface feature (auto-cleanup of pasted text in a dialog) using Eclipse UI framework elements (SWT, JFace, Commands, DND, Handlers). It involves complex control flow, event handling, and custom local variables, which are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern matching and replacement.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would require significant extensions to: 1. Match and replace multi-statement code blocks (including try-finally constructs). 2. Dynamically inject and manage local variables and their lifecycles (e.g., 'Clipboard clipboard = new Clipboard(...)'). 3. Support UI-specific API interactions and event handler registration (e.g., 'control.addDisposeListener').
summary: This commit implements a new UI feature that automatically cleans up pasted text in the Open Type dialog. The core change involves adding a complex block of code within 'createButtonsForButtonBar' to handle clipboard operations, call an internal utility method ('TypeInfoFilter.simplifySearchText'), and manage command handlers. This transformation is highly specific to Eclipse UI infrastructure, involves multi-statement logic with control flow, and introduces new functionality rather than refactoring existing patterns. Therefore, it is classified as RED and cannot be implemented with the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 577182: [16] Provide option in New Record Creation Wizard to create
stubs for the main function.

Change-Id: I8010d9cb690ac7b4a42db10266df8b0dd2f7743b

Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Sarika Sinha <sarika.sinha@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: This change refactors the internal implementation of JDT's new type wizards, specifically moving a method generation logic to a superclass helper method. This is an internal architectural change within the JDT codebase.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL cannot effectively match and replace an arbitrary multi-line block of code (like the 'StringBuilder' logic for creating a method) with a single method call. It lacks the capability to understand the semantic equivalence of such a large structural transformation or to infer the target method call from the removed block's functionality.
summary: This commit refactors JDT's new type wizard logic by extracting the 'main' method generation code from 'NewClassWizardPage.java' into a new protected helper method 'createMainMethod' in its superclass 'NewTypeWizardPage.java'. It then updates 'NewRecordWizardPage.java' to offer an option to create this 'main' method. The core change is a structural refactoring: moving a complex, multi-statement code block into a new method and replacing the original block with a call to that method. This is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression or simple statement replacements. Thus, it is classified as RED.

The rules use `inClass` and `inMethod` guards for precise contextual application.

commitMessage:
Bug 577840 fixed equals

Change-Id: Ie686b0ee0ba3e192aad82c4d1625626434116cf7
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/188952
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The change fixes a logical error in an equals method, specifically making a previously incorrect conditional expression evaluate correctly.
languageChangeNeeded: The rule targets a logical bug in a specific 'equals' method implementation. The safety and correctness of the transformation critically depend on the preceding 'if (this == obj) return true;' statement, which guarantees that 'obj != this' is true if the code reaches the problematic line. The current DSL lacks the ability to define rule applicability based on the semantic consequence of preceding control flow statements (e.g., 'only if a previous return statement was executed'). A simple textual replacement of the condition 'obj == null || obj != this' to 'obj == null' would be semantically unsafe if applied generally, as 'obj != this' is not always trivially true outside this specific context. Therefore, safely and generally implementing this fix requires advanced control flow analysis or DSL extensions to capture such context-dependent logic.
summary: This commit fixes a logical bug in an 'equals' method by simplifying the conditional expression 'if (obj == null || obj!=this)' to 'if (obj == null)'. The original condition was flawed given the preceding 'if (this == obj) return true;' check, effectively causing 'equals' to incorrectly return false for all non-self non-null objects. While the textual change is simple, safely generalizing this rule requires advanced control flow analysis beyond the current DSL's capabilities, as it depends on the precise execution context of the 'equals' method to maintain semantic correctness. Thus, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:

Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit focuses on refactoring internal JDT logic for finding enum constructors, which is a specific internal mechanism.
languageChangeNeeded: The changes involve introducing a new enum, adding a new private method, modifying complex conditional logic (if and switch statements), and introducing conditional assignments based on method properties (isVarargs, getElementType). These are structural and control-flow modifications that are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern matching and replacement.
summary: This commit refactors the internal JDT `EnumConstructorTargetFinder` to add support for varargs and assignment-compatible arguments in enum constructor searches. This involves extensive changes to conditional logic, introduction of a new internal enum, and creation of a new private helper method with complex type matching logic. These modifications are highly structural and control-flow heavy, making them beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations.

categoryReason: The commit specifically addresses a bug in an existing JDT cleanup ('Use Autoboxing'), aiming to fix incorrect behavior rather than introduce a new transformation pattern for user code.
languageChangeNeeded: The core changes involve adding complex semantic guards to existing cleanup logic. These guards require inspecting parent AST nodes, resolving method/constructor bindings, cloning and manipulating type binding arrays, and invoking a complex helper method (`hasConflictingMethodOrConstructor`) to check for overload conflicts. This level of semantic analysis and conditional control flow is beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL's guard functions.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the 'Use Autoboxing' and 'Use Unboxing' JDT cleanups that caused incorrect program logic changes, particularly with method overloading. The fix introduces sophisticated internal guards that prevent the cleanup from applying if it would alter method/constructor resolution. This involves adding new conditional logic, resolving method bindings, and performing complex type compatibility checks, which are too structural and semantically rich for the current TriggerPattern DSL.

categoryReason: The change is an internal architectural refactoring within a JDT UI component, replacing one internal mechanism with another.
languageChangeNeeded: The transformation involves removing an existing field (`fNotifierJob`), adding a new field (`throttledUpdates`) with a complex initializer (including a method reference), and replacing a method call within a method's body. These structural modifications (field additions/removals, changes to complex field initializers, and complex block replacements) are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL.

This is an internal architectural change involving the removal of an existing field and its logic, and the introduction of a new field with a complex initializer and corresponding method call replacement. These structural changes and complex field initializations are not directly expressible using the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 576733 - avoid synchronization in ProblemMarkerManager

to avoid locking other threads.

Change-Id: I58ca413df8daed8de2de4f93053fb6181e12d8f7
Signed-off-by: Joerg Kubitz <jkubitz-eclipse@gmx.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/186656
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit's primary goal is to address concurrency and avoid synchronization locks, fitting into the 'Concurrency' category which currently exists in the `concurrency.sandbox-hint` file (though no rules for this specific pattern exist yet).
languageChangeNeeded:
The commit involves several complex structural changes:
1.  **Changing field declarations**: Converting non-final `Set` fields to `final ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet()` and `final ListenerList<>()` directly at declaration site. The DSL does not have specific patterns for matching and replacing entire field declarations and their initializers.
2.  **Removing constructor body**: Deleting lines from a constructor is a structural code removal, not an expression or statement replacement.
3.  **Replacing `synchronized` blocks with direct thread-safe collection operations**: This involves complex control flow (removing `synchronized`, `if (!changedElements.isEmpty())`, `if (fResourcesWithMarkerChanges.isEmpty()) / else`) and replacing it with a single `fResourcesWithMarkerChanges.addAll()` call. The DSL cannot perform such large-scale structural replacements of control flow constructs.
4.  **Refactoring `runPendingUpdates` method**: This part introduces new local `ArrayList` variables, uses `removeIf` (which involves a lambda), and `toArray(IResource[]::new)` (a method reference, potentially Java 11+). This is a complex multi-statement rewrite that alters fundamental logic and resource handling within a method, which is beyond the current DSL's capabilities for expression/statement replacement.
summary: This commit refactors `ProblemMarkerManager` to avoid synchronization and improve concurrency. It replaces non-final `Set` fields with `final ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet()` and `ListenerList` initialized at declaration, removes constructor-based initialization, and replaces complex synchronized blocks with direct, thread-safe collection operations. These changes are highly structural, involve modifying field declarations, complex control flow, and introducing new internal logic with lambdas and method references. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for pattern matching and replacement of expressions and simple statements, not for such deep architectural refactorings and control flow manipulation. Therefore, this change is not implementable with the current DSL capabilities.

commitMessage:
Bug 578038 - [18][javadoc] provide support for @highlight markup in
@snippet

Change-Id: I906631d01c72c5f62e7b728a7b879f50934aabe3
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/189929
categoryReason: This commit introduces new code generation logic specific to Javadoc processing and the `@snippet`/`@highlight` tags, which is a specialized area not covered by existing categories.
languageChangeNeeded:
This commit introduces entirely new functionality to parse and process the `@highlight` Javadoc tag within `@snippet` blocks (a Java 18 feature). This involves:
1.  **Adding new methods**: The commit adds multiple new private helper methods (`handleSnippetHighlight`, `getHighlightHtmlTag`, `arePropertyValuesStringLiterals`, `getHighlightPropertyValue`, `stripQuotes`). The DSL cannot 'add new methods' with arbitrary complex logic.
2.  **Complex control flow**: The new methods contain intricate `if/else` branching, `while` loops, and `switch` statements, along with conditional logic involving string checks (`length()`, `charAt()`, `equals()`). The DSL is limited to single-expression or simple statement transformations, not arbitrary control flow insertion.
3.  **Regular expression processing**: The `handleSnippetHighlight` method uses `java.util.regex.Pattern.compile()` and `java.util.regex.Matcher` for dynamic regex matching and replacement. The DSL does not support embedding or generating such complex regex processing logic.
4.  **`StringBuilder` manipulation**: The method constructs new strings using `StringBuilder`, which is a procedural API usage pattern not amenable to simple DSL pattern-replacement.

While the commit introduces Java 18-specific Javadoc tags, the core issue is that it's introducing _new code implementation_ rather than refactoring existing code patterns via simple substitutions.
summary: This commit adds new functionality to JDT's Javadoc rendering to support `@highlight` markup within `@snippet` tags (a Java 18 feature). It involves introducing several new private helper methods, each containing complex procedural logic, control flow structures (if/else, while loops, switch statements), regular expression processing (`Pattern`/`Matcher`), and `StringBuilder` manipulation. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for declarative pattern matching and replacement of existing code structures, not for generating or embedding arbitrary new Java method implementations with complex control flow and logic. Therefore, this change is not implementable with the current DSL capabilities.

commitMessage:
Bug 558292 - Offer quick fix to extract a lambda body to a method

- add new method getExtractMethodFromLambdaProposal() to
  QuickAssistProcessor
- add new message to CorrectionMessages
- add new relevance value EXTRACT_LAMBDA_BODY_TO_METHOD to
  IProposalRelevance
- add new tests to AssistQuickFixText1d8

Change-Id: I749fdce7702fa2ff2a656e379534bc518abc0365
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/188802
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit introduces a new quick-fix feature to extract a lambda body to a method. This is an extension to JDT's quick-assist functionality.
languageChangeNeeded: The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for pattern matching and replacement of expressions, statements, and annotations. The 'Extract Method' refactoring is a complex, multi-step structural transformation that involves: 1. Analyzing local variables and context. 2. Generating a new method. 3. Replacing the original code block with a call to the new method. 4. Handling potential name conflicts. This level of semantic analysis and arbitrary code generation (especially new method declarations with dynamic parameters) is beyond the current capabilities of the DSL. While the DSL can call a fix function, the fix function itself would contain all the imperative Java logic, meaning the transformation isn't expressed *in the DSL*.
summary: This commit adds a new quick-fix feature to extract a lambda body to a method. This involves adding new UI constants and implementing a complex 'Extract Method' refactoring logic in Java within the `QuickAssistProcessor` class. The TriggerPattern DSL is not capable of performing such complex structural refactorings that involve generating new methods, analyzing variable scope for parameters, and replacing arbitrary blocks of code with method calls. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED. The `sandbox_triggerpattern` plugin is listed as it's the general host for DSL-based transformations, but the specific 'Extract Method' functionality would require an imperative Java plugin.

commitMessage:
Bug 578039 - [18][javadoc] provide support for @replace markup in
@snippet

Change-Id: Ied916974fbad5688cb036d7b41f0309da4274466
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190092
summary: This commit adds support for the '@replace' markup within Javadoc '@snippet' tags (Java 18+). It involves renaming an internal helper method 'getHighlightPropertyValue' to 'getPropertyValue' and updating its call sites, and modifying a boolean expression in the 'stripQuotes' method from 'str.length() > 2' to 'str.length() >= 2' for robustness. These are direct 1:1 method call and expression replacements, fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL. Other changes, such as adding try-catch blocks and moving/duplicating complex logic between methods, are structural and not expressible in the current DSL. The two identified patterns are highly reusable for internal JDT API modernization and are classified as GREEN. The minimum Java version for the hint file is 18 due to the context of supporting Java 18 Javadoc features.

commitMessage:
Bug 578370 - [18][javadoc] provide support for property values to be
wrapped in single quotes for @highlight in snippets

Change-Id: I70cd2454e30913535b869d4fc699e0d6ff3f756c
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190269
categoryReason: This change modifies internal JDT UI code related to Javadoc processing. It's a targeted refactoring for JDT's own codebase.
summary: This commit fixes Bug 578370 by enhancing an internal JDT Javadoc processing utility method (`stripQuotes`) to correctly handle property values wrapped in single quotes in addition to double quotes. The rule replaces a boolean condition `str.charAt(0) == '"' && str.charAt(length-1) == '"'` with an extended version that includes an OR clause for single quotes. This is a direct 1:1 expression replacement within a method, fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule with `inClass` and `inMethod` guards for precision. The change improves the robustness of JDT's internal Javadoc parsing.

commitMessage:
Bug 578527 - Move substring cleanup to jdt.core.manipulation

Change-Id: I4b3becca1ff4756d58535791e26eb645cc29a5f1
Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190362
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
irrelevantReason: This commit is an architectural refactoring, moving an existing cleanup implementation (`SubstringCleanUp`) between internal JDT plugins (`org.eclipse.jdt.ui` to `org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation`). It does not introduce a new transformation pattern for user code, but rather reorganizes existing cleanup logic. The underlying cleanup functionality, which simplifies `String.substring(startIndex, str.length())` to `String.substring(startIndex)`, is already covered by an existing DSL rule and is considered a Java-based plugin.
categoryReason: The primary change involves moving and refactoring existing code between modules rather than introducing a new code transformation pattern.
summary: This commit is an architectural refactoring to move the `SubstringCleanUp` implementation from `org.eclipse.jdt.ui` to `org.eclipse.jdt.core.manipulation`. The commit reorganizes the class to delegate to a new `SubstringCleanUpCore`. This change does not introduce a new user-facing code transformation pattern. The functionality of the `SubstringCleanUp` (simplifying `substring($start, $str.length())` to `substring($start)`) is already covered by a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule in `string-modernization.sandbox-hint` (`java.lang.String.$str.substring($start, $str.length()) :: hasNoSideEffect($str) => $str.substring($start) ;;`), and `SubstringCleanUp` itself exists as a Java-based plugin. Thus, this commit is irrelevant for new DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Bug 450663 - UI freeze due
ProblemsLabelDecorator.getErrorTicksFromMarkers

Introduced shared adornment cache and asynchronous resource decoration
calculation for JDT ProblemsLabelDecorator. This avoids performing most
of potentially long blocking resource operations on UI thread and saves
many repeating marker query operations (both via
IResource.findMaxProblemSeverity()).

Change-Id: I0011a790dd0b06eb7649cc462a639d49ccf41f7c
Signed-off-by: Andrey Loskutov <loskutov@gmx.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190484
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: This commit involves major structural and architectural changes within JDT UI components, which cannot be expressed by current DSL patterns.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires extensive DSL extensions for structural code changes: introducing new classes, adding fields to existing classes, modifying method signatures, wrapping existing logic in new control flow, and managing asynchronous job scheduling. The current DSL is primarily for expression/statement-level pattern matching and replacement.
summary: This commit represents a major internal architectural refactoring of JDT's `ProblemsLabelDecorator` to improve UI performance by offloading problem marker calculations to background jobs and implementing a caching mechanism. It introduces multiple new internal classes, modifies existing method signatures, and alters complex control flow. These changes are deeply structural and cannot be expressed as pattern-based DSL rules.

commitMessage:
Bug 450663 - don't check IResource.isAccessible() on UI if possible

The same check will be done later in the job, so we can avoid to check
that in the UI in computeContainerAdornmentFlags() to avoid random UI
freezes.

Change-Id: I3e894a953081e80293f61493eed6427fd6c6fa4a
Signed-off-by: Andrey Loskutov <loskutov@gmx.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190496
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: This is an internal code optimization within a JDT UI component to improve responsiveness.
summary: This commit optimizes the internal `computeContainerAdornmentFlags` method in `ProblemsLabelDecorator.java` by removing a redundant `!resource.isAccessible()` check from an `if` condition. This prevents unnecessary blocking calls on the UI thread, improving responsiveness. The transformation is a direct 1:1 boolean expression replacement and is fully implementable as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule, guarded by `inClass` and `inMethod` to ensure precise application to the internal JDT UI code.
